title: ipomopsis congesta subsp. crebrifolia
intro: Ipomopsis congesta subsp. crebrifolia, also known as crevice gilia, is a species of flowering plant in the phlox family that is native to the western United States and Canada. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe sore throats and wounds. The health benefits of crevice gilia include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and promote respiratory health. Crevice gilia can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, making it a versatile and effective natural remedy for various ailments.
